description: Roadmap #7 — the governance surface is itself budgeted. A per-category cap on the framework's own enforcement machinery (gates, CLI verbs, audit checks) so it can't grow faster than dead surface retires — the cure for F3/F4 becoming the next F3/F4. To exceed a cap, either retire/merge something OR add a waiver row here with a reason (each waiver raises that category's effective cap by exactly one, and shows as visible debt in `maddu audit`). Counts are read from ground truth (discoverGates / bin COMMANDS / the audit's own check registry) — nothing is hand-maintained. Enforced by checkGovernanceBudget in commands/audit.mjs via template/maddu/runtime/lib/governance-budget.mjs.

gates.note: builtin enforcement gates (template/maddu/runtime/gates/builtin/*.mjs). Raised 71→72 (roadmap #12c phase 3): +replica-untracked, which asserts a team-sync checkout's .maddu/config/replica.json is never git-tracked (a committed replicaId duplicates across every clone and resurrects the multi-writer conflict — the single most dangerous sync misconfiguration). A deliberate, attributed raise for a permanent, warranted enforcement surface — not temporary debt (which would be a waiver). Nothing retired: the sync-identity invariant has no existing home. Prior: 70→71 (v1.94.0, #12b phase 7) +event-schema-complete.

verbs.note: top-level CLI verbs (bin/maddu.mjs COMMANDS). Raised 70->71 (EXP roadmap phase 3, 2026-07-06): +evolve, the recommend-only evolution planner (`plan` derives evidence-backed recommendations from the experience projection; `adopt` is the explicit operator apply routed through EXISTING write paths). The companion `experience` verb (EXP phase 1) consumed the last headroom under the old cap, so this raise is attributed to the EXP capability pair as a permanent, warranted surface — flagged in the phase-0 design doc BEFORE the build, not discovered at gate time. Nothing retired: no existing verb covers evidence-backed self-improvement recommendations.

audit-checks.note: maddu audit coherence checks (reusable gates surfaced + audit-only checks)

selfTest.note: relative self-test latency ceiling. WARN (never FAIL) when the last recorded quick/full run in .maddu/state/self-test-last-run.json exceeds baselineMs * (1 + tolerancePct/100). Raise the baseline deliberately when the suite legitimately grows. Raised 36000→48000 then →115000 across v1.93.0 (roadmap #12a phases 1-5). Attribution (measured, not laundered): (a) `maddu ci` grew 5.4s→8.7s from the #12 surface additions (2 new event types, 3 new runtime libs, more source for the architecture gates) and the ci-command self-test invokes `maddu ci` 7× sequentially, so that +3.3s amplifies to ~+23s; (b) the two git-spawning integration tests worktree-attach + worktree-detach add ~11s; (c) the suite runs sequentially so per-test costs sum. The dominant driver is ci-command's 7× ci pattern — a candidate to parallelize or thin in a future pass.
